title: Termination, Budgets, Timeouts, and Progress Detection · Incident Triage
summary: Stop an agent safely under success, failure, budget, and no-progress conditions. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a loop termination and budget policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Terminate only when completion evidence satisfies the goal contract.
Classify retryable, recoverable, authority-blocked, and terminal failures.
Budget turns, tokens, time, tool calls, money, and external side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Publish the incident record

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop with a resumable state, evidence, and explicit unmet conditions.
